sections.title: 2022 AMENDMENT
sections.text: The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty -fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.
sections.retrieval_text:
2022 AMENDMENT
level=1
The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty -fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.

sections.citation: bb5f2997-19a7-4d51-82ed-a76deaf3c12c, PAGE LOCATION TEXT IN PREVIOUS VERSION TEXT IN AMENDED VERSION, p. 2
sections.text: 5 Section 7.4 Labelling of Non-Retail Containers Information, as appropriate needed for labelling of retail containers is given either on the nonretail container or in accompanying documents except that the name of the food, date marking and storage instructions, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the nonretail container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

5 Section 7.4 Labelling of Non-Retail Containers Information, as appropriate needed for labelling of retail containers is given either on the nonretail container or in accompanying documents except that the name of the food, date marking and storage instructions, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the nonretail container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to products designated as "Luncheon Meat" which have been packed in any suitable packing material.
sections.retrieval_text:
SCOPE
number=1
level=1
This standard applies to products designated as "Luncheon Meat" which have been packed in any suitable packing material.

page_spans.text: This standard applies to products designated as "Luncheon Meat" which have been packed in any suitable packing material.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: The product shall be prepared from meat or poultry meat or a combination of these as defined below which has been comminuted and cured and which may have been smoked. The product may or may not contain binders. The heat treatment to which the product has been subjected and the type of cure and packaging shall be sufficient to ensure that the product presents no public health hazard and remains wholesome under the conditions of storage, transport and sale.
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION
number=2
level=1
The product shall be prepared from meat or poultry meat or a combination of these as defined below which has been comminuted and cured and which may have been smoked. The product may or may not contain binders. The heat treatment to which the product has been subjected and the type of cure and packaging shall be sufficient to ensure that the product presents no public health hazard and remains wholesome under the conditions of storage, transport and sale.

page_spans.text: The product shall be prepared from meat or poultry meat or a combination of these as defined below which has been comminuted and cured and which may have been smoked. The product may or may not contain binders. The heat treatment to which the product has been subjected and the type of cure and packaging shall be sufficient to ensure that the product presents no public health hazard and remains wholesome under the conditions of storage, transport and sale.

sections.title: ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
number=3
level=1

sections.title: Subsidiary Definitions
sections.text: For the purpose of this standard: - Edible offal means such offals as have been passed as fit for human consumption including lungs (but not if the animal from which the lungs have been taken has been scalded by immersion in hot water) but not including ears, scalp, snouts (including lips and muzzle), mucous membrane, sinews, genital system, udders, intestines and urinary bladder. Edible offal also includes poultry skin. - Meat means the edible part including edible offal from any mammal slaughtered in an abattoir. - Packaged means packed in a container manufactured of materials which do not permit contamination under normal conditions of handling. - Poultry meat means the edible part of any domesticated birds including chickens, turkeys, ducks, geese, guinea-fowls or pigeons slaughtered in an abattoir.
sections.retrieval_text:

For the purpose of this standard: - Edible offal means such offals as have been passed as fit for human consumption including lungs (but not if the animal from which the lungs have been taken has been scalded by immersion in hot water) but not including ears, scalp, snouts (including lips and muzzle), mucous membrane, sinews, genital system, udders, intestines and urinary bladder. Edible offal also includes poultry skin. - Meat means the edible part including edible offal from any mammal slaughtered in an abattoir. - Packaged means packed in a container manufactured of materials which do not permit contamination under normal conditions of handling. - Poultry meat means the edible part of any domesticated birds including chickens, turkeys, ducks, geese, guinea-fowls or pigeons slaughtered in an abattoir.

page_spans.text: For the purpose of this standard: - Edible offal means such offals as have been passed as fit for human consumption including lungs (but not if the animal from which the lungs have been taken has been scalded by immersion in hot water) but not including ears, scalp, snouts (including lips and muzzle), mucous membrane, sinews, genital system, udders, intestines and urinary bladder. Edible offal also includes poultry skin. - Meat means the edible part including edible offal from any mammal slaughtered in an abattoir. - Packaged means packed in a container manufactured of materials which do not permit contamination under normal conditions of handling. - Poultry meat means the edible part of any domesticated birds including chickens, turkeys, ducks, geese, guinea-fowls or pigeons slaughtered in an abattoir.

sections.title: Essential Ingredients
sections.text: - Meat or poultry meat or a combination of these excluding edible offal; - Water; - Curing ingredients consisting of food-grade salt and sodium or potassium nitrite.
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS > Essential Ingredients
number=3.1
level=2
- Meat or poultry meat or a combination of these excluding edible offal; - Water; - Curing ingredients consisting of food-grade salt and sodium or potassium nitrite.

page_spans.text: - Meat or poultry meat or a combination of these excluding edible offal; - Water; - Curing ingredients consisting of food-grade salt and sodium or potassium nitrite.

sections.title: Optional Ingredients
sections.text: - Edible offal, fat per se, cured and uncured pork rind per se; - Carbohydrate and protein binders such as: meal, flour or starch prepared from grain, or potato or sweet potato; bread, biscuit or bakery products; milk powder, skim milk powder, butter milk powder, caseinate, whey powder, egg protein, dried blood products, vegetable protein products; - Sucrose, invert sugar, dextrose (glucose), lactose, maltose, glucose syrup (including corn syrup); - Spices, seasonings and condiments; - Water soluble, aromatic hydrolysed protein.

sections.title: Composition
sections.text: Product with binder Product without binder and edible offal (but may include heart, tongue or head meat from mammals) Minimum ingoing meat content 80% 90% Maximum fat content 35% 30%

sections.title: ESSENTIAL QUALITY FACTORS
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL QUALITY FACTORS
number=3.4
level=1

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Preservatives, humectants and colours used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in food category 08.3.2 “Heat-treated processed commi nuted meat, poultry, and game products ” and its parent food categories are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard. Only certain Table 3 food additives (as indicated in Table 3) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard. Use of flavouring substances should be consistent with the Guidelines for the Use of Flavourings (CXG 66-2008). Section 4.1 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995), referring to the conditions applying to carry-over of food additives from ingredients and raw materials into foods, shall apply.

level=1

page_spans.text: Preservatives, humectants and colours used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in food category 08.3.2 “Heat-treated processed commi nuted meat, poultry, and game products ” and its parent food categories are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard. Only certain Table 3 food additives (as indicated in Table 3) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard. Use of flavouring substances should be consistent with the Guidelines for the Use of Flavourings (CXG 66-2008). Section 4.1 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995), referring to the conditions applying to carry-over of food additives from ingredients and raw materials into foods, shall apply.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the Maximum Levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides and/or veterinary drugs established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

page_spans.text: The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the Maximum Levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The products covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides and/or veterinary drugs established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared an d handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Meat (CXC 58-2005), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low-Acid and Acidified Low-Acid Canned Foods (CXC 23-1979), the Guidelines on the Application of General Principles of Food Hygiene to the Control of Listeria monocytogenes in Ready-to-Eat Foods (CXG 61-2007) and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: LABELLING
sections.text: The provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), shall apply:

LABELLING
number=7
level=1
The provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), shall apply:

page_spans.text: The provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), shall apply:

sections.title: Raw Material
sections.text: The ingredients from which the product is prepared shall be of a quality suitable for human consumption and free from objectionable odours and flavours.
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL QUALITY FACTORS > Raw Material
number=3.4.1
level=2
The ingredients from which the product is prepared shall be of a quality suitable for human consumption and free from objectionable odours and flavours.

page_spans.text: The ingredients from which the product is prepared shall be of a quality suitable for human consumption and free from objectionable odours and flavours.

sections.title: Final Product
sections.text: The product shall be clean and substantially free from staining and contamination from the container. The meat and poultry meat shall be uniformly and thoroughly cured and the product shall be capable of being sliced.
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL QUALITY FACTORS > Final Product
number=3.4.2
level=2
The product shall be clean and substantially free from staining and contamination from the container. The meat and poultry meat shall be uniformly and thoroughly cured and the product shall be capable of being sliced.

page_spans.text: The product shall be clean and substantially free from staining and contamination from the container. The meat and poultry meat shall be uniformly and thoroughly cured and the product shall be capable of being sliced.

sections.title: The Name of the Food
sections.text: The name of the food to be declared on the label shall be "Luncheon Meat". A declaration of the presence of binders and of edible offal and a declaration indicating the species of animal from which the meat, poultry meat or a combination of these is derived shall be given in connection with the name of the product if their omission would mislead the consumer.

sections.title: List of Ingredients
sections.text: The list of ingredients shall indicate the species of animals from which the meat, poultry meat or a combination of these is derived.

sections.title: Date Marking and Storage Instructions
sections.text: For shelf-stable products the date of minimum durability shall be declared by the year. For products which are not shelf-stable i.e. which may be expected not to keep for at least 18 months in normal conditions of storage and sale, and which are packaged in a container ready for offer to the consumer or for catering purposes, the date of minimum durability shall be declared by day, month and year. For products which are not shelf-stable and which are packaged in containers not sold directly to the consumer or for catering purposes, adequate storage and distribution instructions shall be declared.
sections.retrieval_text:
7.3 DATE MARKING AND STORAGE INSTRUCTIONS
number=7.3
level=1
For shelf-stable products the date of minimum durability shall be declared by the year. For products which are not shelf-stable i.e. which may be expected not to keep for at least 18 months in normal conditions of storage and sale, and which are packaged in a container ready for offer to the consumer or for catering purposes, the date of minimum durability shall be declared by day, month and year. For products which are not shelf-stable and which are packaged in containers not sold directly to the consumer or for catering purposes, adequate storage and distribution instructions shall be declared.

page_spans.text: For shelf-stable products the date of minimum durability shall be declared by the year. For products which are not shelf-stable i.e. which may be expected not to keep for at least 18 months in normal conditions of storage and sale, and which are packaged in a container ready for offer to the consumer or for catering purposes, the date of minimum durability shall be declared by day, month and year. For products which are not shelf-stable and which are packaged in containers not sold directly to the consumer or for catering purposes, adequate storage and distribution instructions shall be declared.

sections.title: Labelling of Non-Retail Containers
sections.text: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

level=1

page_spans.text: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: METHODS OF ANALYSIS
sections.text: For checking the compliance with this Standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this Standard shall be used.

classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

sentences.original: This standard applies to products designated as "Luncheon Meat" which have been packed in any suitable packing material.

sentences.original: The product shall be prepared from meat or poultry meat or a combination of these as defined below which has been comminuted and cured and which may have been smoked.

classification.sentence: The heat treatment to which the product has been subjected and the type of cure and packaging shall be sufficient to ensure that the product presents no public health hazard and remains wholesome under the conditions of storage, transport and sale.
classification.function: Requirement for treatment sufficiency ensuring safety and wholesomeness
classification.pattern: Complex sentence with obligation modal 'shall' expressing sufficiency condition and result clauses

applicability_conditions: under conditions of storage, transport and sale

classification.justification: Complex obligation linking treatment sufficiency to safety and wholesomeness outcomes; no existing template fully covers this causal structure.
notes: Includes causative clause and outcome specification uncommon in simple obligation templates.

sentences.original: For the purpose of this standard: - Edible offal means such offals as have been passed as fit for human consumption including lungs (but not if the animal from which the lungs have been taken has been scalded by immersion in hot water) but not including ears, scalp, snouts (including lips and muzzle), mucous membrane, sinews, genital system, udders, intestines and urinary bladder.

sentences.original: - Edible offal, fat per se, cured and uncured pork rind per se; - Carbohydrate and protein binders such as: meal, flour or starch prepared from grain, or potato or sweet potato; bread, biscuit or bakery products; milk powder, skim milk powder, butter milk powder, caseinate, whey powder, egg protein, dried blood products, vegetable protein products; - Sucrose, invert sugar, dextrose (glucose), lactose, maltose, glucose syrup (including corn syrup); - Spices, seasonings and condiments; - Water soluble, aromatic hydrolysed protein.

sentences.original: Preservatives, humectants and colours used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in food category 08.3.2 “Heat-treated processed commi nuted meat, poultry, and game products ” and its parent food categories are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard.

sentences.original: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared an d handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Meat (CXC 58-2005), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low-Acid and Acidified Low-Acid Canned Foods (CXC 23-1979), the Guidelines on the Application of General Principles of Food Hygiene to the Control of Listeria monocytogenes in Ready-to-Eat Foods (CXG 61-2007) and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR LUNCHEON MEAT
pages.summary: Página com título principal do documento 'STANDARD FOR LUNCHEON MEAT' e cabeçalho 'CXS 89-1981'. Sem seções estruturais detectadas nesta página, apenas informações de revisão e adoção presentes.

Página contém cabeçalho 'CXS 89-1981  2' e duas seções principais sem numeração que funcionam como títulos em caixa alta: '2022 AMENDMENT' e uma seção longa com título misto em caixa alta. Texto interno está associado conforme subsequente aos títulos. Sem continuidade entre páginas.

Página contém cabeçalho repetitivo 'CXS 89-1981  3' e três seções principais numeradas de nível 1: '1. SCOPE', '2. DESCRIPTION', '3. ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS'. A seção 2 tem subtítulo 2.1 de nível 2. A seção 3 tem três subtítulos numerados (3.1, 3.2, 3.3) classificados como nível 2. Cada título tem texto associado conforme presente na página. Não há continuidade entre páginas.

Página contém as seções principais numeradas de nível 1: 3.4, 4, 5, 6 e 7. A seção 3.4 inclui subtítulos 3.4.1 e 3.4.2 de nível 2. A seção 7 inclui subtítulos 7.1 e 7.2 de nível 2. Texto associado foi atribuído corretamente a cada nó. A seção 3.4 está marcada como continuação da página anterior. A subseção 7.1 está marcada como continua na próxima página. Títulos aparecem visualmente na página.

Página contém 3 seções principais numeradas 7.3, 7.4 e 8 de nível 1, cada uma com seu texto associado. Não há subtítulos nesta página. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado. Nenhuma seção continua da página anterior ou para a próxima. Títulos aparecem visualmente na página.
